title: Adding Sign in with Google
description:
## Social Login

Product wants a **"Sign in with Google"** button on your login page so end users can authenticate with their Google account.

What do you do in Keycloak?

options.text: Configure **Google** as an **Identity Provider** (a built-in social provider), supplying the OAuth **client ID and client secret** from Google Cloud
Set up **LDAP user federation** pointed at Google
Create a brand-new **realm** dedicated to Google users
Manually create a Keycloak user for every Google account that might sign in

explanation: Keycloak ships **built-in social identity providers** (Google, GitHub, Microsoft, Facebook, and more). You add **Google** under Identity Providers and paste the **client ID and client secret** obtained from a Google Cloud OAuth credential; Keycloak then shows a 'Sign in with Google' option and brokers the login, creating/linking a local account on first sign-in. (a) is correct. (b) is wrong — Google isn't an LDAP store you bind to; social login is identity brokering over OAuth/OIDC. (c) is unnecessary and wrong — a new realm doesn't add Google login. (d) defeats the purpose of social login entirely.

hints.title: It's just an identity provider
hints.body: Social login is identity brokering with a preconfigured provider. Add Google under 'Identity Providers' and fill in the client ID/secret from your Google OAuth app.
